Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Let me carefully analyze this event log to identify performance issues and root causes

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Step 1 Calculate case durations

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Let me calculate the total duration for each case

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 2001 Submit 2024 04 01 09 00 Close 2024 04 01 10 30 1 5 hours 90 minutes Case 2002 Submit 2024 04 01 09 05 Close 2024 04 02 11 00 26 hours Case 2003 Submit 2024 04 01 09 10 Close 2024 04 03 09 30 48 hours 20 minutes Case 2004 Submit 2024 04 01 09 20 Close 2024 04 01 10 45 1 hour 25 minutes 85 minutes Case 2005 Submit 2024 04 01 09 25 Close 2024 04 04 14 30 77 hours over 3 days

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: So the cases by duration Case 2004 85 minutes shortest Case 2001 90 minutes Case 2002 26 hours Case 2003 48 hours Case 2005 77 hours longest Cases 2002 2003 and 2005 are taking significantly longer

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Step 2 Analyze attributes

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Let me look at the attributes of each case

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Observations Complexity is the strongest differentiator Low complexity 2001 2004 85 90 min no document requests Medium complexity 2002 26 hrs 1 document request High complexity 2003 2005 48 77 hrs 2 3 document requests

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Region Region A Cases 2001 Low 90 min 2003 High 48 hrs Region B Cases 2004 Low 85 min 2002 Medium 26 hrs 2005 High 77 hrs When comparing same complexity Region As high case 2003 is faster 48 hrs than Region Bs high case 2005 77 hrs However the difference might be due to number of doc requests more than region

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Resources Manager Ann handles Low and Medium cases fast Manager Bill handles High cases slower Adjuster Lisa handles all the document requests in long cases 2002 2003 2005 Adjuster Mike also handles document requests in case 2003

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Multiple Document Requests correlation Case 2005 3 requests over 3 days Case 2003 2 requests gap of 6 hours between them Case 2002 1 request with 19 plus hour gap Cases 2001 2004 0 requests

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Step 3 Root cause analysis The data shows that Complexity is the primary driver of duration High complexity leads to multiple document requests Each request adds significant wait time The waiting time between document requests and approval is the main bottleneck Adjuster Lisa seems to handle the bulk of document request follow ups possibly indicating workload Region B seems to have slightly longer high complexity cases but the data is limited

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Let me also calculate the waiting time between activities for the long cases

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 2002 Medium B Submit 09 05 Evaluate 09 45 40 min Evaluate 09 45 Request 14 00 4 hr 15 min Request 14 00 Approve next day 10 00 20 hours Approve 10 00 Pay 10 45 45 min Pay 10 45 Close 11 00 15 min

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 2003 High A Submit 09 10 Evaluate 09 40 30 min Evaluate 09 40 Request 11 00 1 hr 20 min Request 11 00 Request 17 00 6 hours Request 17 00 Approve next day 16 00 23 hours Approve 16 00 Pay next day 09 00 17 hours Pay 09 00 Close 09 30 30 min

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 2005 High B Submit 09 25 Evaluate 09 50 25 min Evaluate 09 50 Request 11 30 1 hr 40 min Request 11 30 Request next day 17 00 29 5 hours Request 17 00 Request next day 15 00 22 hours Request 15 00 Approve next day 10 00 19 hours Approve 10 00 Pay 14 00 4 hours Pay 14 00 Close 14 30 30 min

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So the main delays are in the document request approval phase

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Resource analysis Adjuster Lisa handles 2002 2004 2005 all Region B Adjuster Mike handles 2001 2003 all Region A All document requests in Region B are by Lisa in Region A by Mike So we cant really say one adjuster is slower they handle different regions But Adjuster Lisa handles more high complexity cases in Region B

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: Manager analysis Manager Ann handles 2001 Low 2002 Medium 2004 Low faster cases Manager Bill handles 2003 High 2005 High slower cases This could be because Bill handles all high complexity cases or because Bill is slower

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Wait let me re examine The approval time itself doesnt seem to be the bottleneck The bottleneck is between the request and approval waiting for customer to provide documents or for review

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Let me make sure my calculations are accurate Case 2001 09 00 to 10 30 1h 30m 90 minutes Case 2002 2024 04 01 09 05 to 2024 04 02 11 00 04 01 09 05 to 04 02 09 05 24 hours 04 02 09 05 to 04 02 11 00 1h 55m Total 25h 55m 26 hours Case 2003 2024 04 01 09 10 to 2024 04 03 09 30 04 01 09 10 to 04 03 09 10 48 hours 04 03 09 10 to 04 03 09 30 20m Total 48h 20m Case 2004 09 20 to 10 45 1h 25m 85 minutes Case 2005 2024 04 01 09 25 to 2024 04 04 14 30 04 01 09 25 to 04 04 09 25 72 hours 04 04 09 25 to 04 04 14 30 5h 5m Total 77h 5m 77 hours Good my calculations are correct
